qa_pairs.question: When using `makeCluster` for R parallel jobs with Slurm, why might all processes be placed on a single node, even if multiple nodes are available?
qa_pairs.answer: The Slurm scheduler might place all processes on just one node by default, even when using `makeCluster` with R, if there are no explicit instructions to distribute them across multiple nodes.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user ensure that R processes leveraging `makeCluster` are distributed across different nodes by the Slurm scheduler?
qa_pairs.answer: To ensure R processes are distributed across different nodes when using `makeCluster` with Slurm, you can add `#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=<number>` to your job script, specifying the desired number of tasks per node.
